pattern: Counting how often each digit character occurs up front turns 'does this digit appear exactly as many times as its own value' into an O(1) lookup while scanning adjacent pairs left to right.
hints: Count how many times each digit '1'..'9' occurs in s before looking at any pair.
Scan adjacent pairs (s[i], s[i+1]) left to right; a pair is only worth checking further if the two characters differ.
A differing pair is valid exactly when each digit's precomputed count equals its own numeric value; return the first such pair as a 2-character string, or "" if the scan finishes with none.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: valid pair in the middle of the string
statement example: valid pair using digits 2 and 1
statement example: no valid pair exists
minimum length, the only possible pair is valid
minimum length, adjacent equal digits can never be valid
an earlier differing pair fails the count check, a later one succeeds
the first differing pair found is not the only one satisfying counts, must return the leftmost

stress.description: random digit strings across the allowed length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 100)))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(1, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
